item.title: 【How to Integrate an AI Chatbot Into Your Application: A Practical Engineering Guide】如何将人工智能聊天机器人集成到您的应用程序中：实用工程指南
item.description:
【

AI chatbots are increasingly part of modern application architectures, not as standalone features but integrated interaction layers. When designed correctly, a chatbot can simplify user workflows, reduce friction, and act as a controlled interface to backend systems.

This guide focuses on how to integrate an AI chatbot into an application from an engineering perspective, covering architecture, implementation steps, and operational considerations without relying on vendor-driven narratives.

】

人工智能聊天机器人越来越多地成为现代应用架构的一部分，不是作为独立的功能，而是集成的交互层。如果设计正确，聊天机器人可以简化用户工作流程、减少摩擦，并充当后端系统的受控界面。

本指南重点介绍如何从工程角度将人工智能聊天机器人集成到应用程序中，涵盖架构、实施步骤和操作注意事项，而不依赖于供应商驱动的叙述。

item.title: 【Integration Reliability for AI Systems: A Framework for Detecting and Preventing Interface Mismatch at Scale】人工智能系统的集成可靠性：大规模检测和防止接口不匹配的框架
item.description:
【

Integration failures inside AI systems rarely appear as dramatic outages. They show up as silent distortions: a schema change that shifts a downstream feature distribution, a latency bump that breaks a timing assumption, or an unexpected enum that slips through because someone pushed a small update without revalidating the contract. 

The underlying services continue to report “healthy.” Dashboards stay green. Pipelines continue producing artefacts. Yet the system behaves differently because components no longer agree on the terms of cooperation. I see this pattern repeatedly across large AI programs, and it has nothing to do with model performance. It is the natural consequence of distributed teams modifying interfaces independently without enforced boundaries.

】

人工智能系统内部的集成故障很少会表现为严重的中断。它们表现为无声的扭曲：改变下游特征分布的模式更改、打破时序假设的延迟碰撞，或者由于有人在没有重新验证合约的情况下推送小更新而漏掉的意外枚举。 

基础服务继续报告“健康”。仪表板保持绿色。管道继续生产文物。然而，系统的行为有所不同，因为组件不再就合作条款达成一致。我在大型人工智能程序中反复看到这种模式，它与模型性能无关。这是分布式团队在没有强制边界的情况下独立修改接口的自然结果。

item.title: 【Swagger UI in a BFF World: Making Swagger UI Work Natively With BFF Architectures】BFF 世界中的 Swagger UI：使 Swagger UI 与 BFF 架构本地工作
item.description:
【

This article introduces a Swagger UI plugin designed specifically for Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) architectures, along with working demos (with and without OIDC) that validate the approach end to end.

The Rise (and Return) of the BFF

Modern web security has shifted away from storing "tokens in the browser." XSS attacks, stricter browser privacy policies, and evolving OAuth recommendations have made Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) architectures the gold standard for secure web apps.

】

本文介绍了专门为后端换前端 (BFF) 架构设计的 Swagger UI 插件，以及端到端验证该方法的工作演示（带或不带 OIDC）。

BFF 的崛起（和回归）

现代网络安全已经不再存储“浏览器中的令牌”。 XSS 攻击、更严格的浏览器隐私策略以及不断发展的 OAuth 建议使得后端换前端 (BFF) 架构成为安全 Web 应用的黄金标准。

item.title: 【Building Event-Driven Data Pipelines in GCP】在 GCP 中构建事件驱动的数据管道
item.description:
【

The old-fashioned batch processing is not applicable in the current applications. Pipelines need to respond to events in real time when businesses rely on real-time data to track user behavior, to process financial transactions, or to monitor Internet of Things (IoT) devices, instead of hours after the event.

Why Event-Driven Architecture Matters

Event-driven processing versus batch processing is a paradigm shift in the flow of data through the systems. With batch pipelines, data is idle until it is run. In event pipelines, each change is followed by an immediate response. This difference is crucial in the development of fraud detection systems that demand sub-second response times or in systems that offer recommendations that are updated in real-time according to who is currently using it.

】

老式的批处理在当前的应用中已经不适用了。当企业依靠实时数据来跟踪用户行为、处理金融交易或监控物联网 (IoT) 设备时，管道需要实时响应事件，而不是事件发生后数小时。

为什么事件驱动架构很重要

事件驱动处理与批处理相比是系统数据流的范式转变。对于批处理管道，数据在运行之前是空闲的。在事件管道中，每次更改后都会立即响应。这种差异对于开发需要亚秒级响应时间的欺诈检测系统或提供根据当前使用者实时更新的建议的系统至关重要。

item.title: 【The DevSecOps Paradox: Why Security Automation Is Both Solving and Creating Pipeline Vulnerabilities】DevSecOps 悖论：为什么安全自动化既解决又造成管道漏洞
item.description:
【

The numbers tell a troubling story. Forty-five percent of cyberattacks in 2024 exploited weaknesses in CI/CD pipelines, according to industry tracking data. Not application code. Not user credentials. The build and deployment infrastructure itself.

This represents a fundamental shift in how attackers think. Why spend weeks crafting an exploit for production systems when you can compromise the pipeline that deploys to those systems? Poison the well, and every downstream service drinks contaminated water.

】

这些数字讲述了一个令人不安的故事。根据行业跟踪数据，2024 年 45% 的网络攻击利用了 CI/CD 管道中的弱点。不是应用程序代码。不是用户凭据。构建和部署基础设施本身。

这代表了攻击者思维方式的根本转变。当您可以破坏部署到这些系统的管道时，为什么还要花费数周时间为生产系统设计漏洞利用程序呢？给井下毒，所有下游服务都会饮用受污染的水。

item.title: 【Performance-Centric Platform Engineering: Shared Responsibility, Guardrails, and Tenant Isolation】以性能为中心的平台工程：责任共担、护栏和租户隔离
item.description:
【

Platform engineering today isn't just about making deployments faster; it's also about making sure that performance is always predictable, reliable, and scalable. As companies start using internal developer platforms (IDPs) on Kubernetes, performance engineering must shift from being an application-level concern to a platform-embedded discipline.

This is where Performance by Design emerges: a model where responsibility is shared, guardrails are enforced, and tenant isolation ensures fairness and reliability across workloads.

】

当今的平台工程不仅仅在于加快部署速度，还在于加快部署速度。它还要确保性能始终是可预测的、可靠的和可扩展的。随着公司开始在 Kubernetes 上使用内部开发人员平台 (IDP)，性能工程必须从应用程序级问题转变为平台嵌入式学科。

这就是性能设计出现的地方：一种责任共担、强制执行护栏以及租户隔离确保跨工作负载的公平性和可靠性的模型。

item.title: 【How to Extract Document Summaries in C#/.NET】如何在 C#/.NET 中提取文档摘要
item.description:
【

Document Summarization: A Significant Value-Add in the AI Boom

C# developers are often saddled with building document-heavy processing workflows across enterprise systems. When we think about what “document processing” means, we probably focus on things like extraction, validation, conversion, storage, etc., but in many modern systems, being able to quickly parse an understanding of what a document contains is often just as important as parsing data from it.

Whether we’re processing PDFs from an upload workflow, or tapping into an email server to handle inbound email attachments, or even dealing with hand-scanned images from mobile devices, producing short, readable document summaries can drastically improve how downstream systems and users interact with those documents. A concise summary paragraph can help with anything from document approval & review to search indexing or triage (among many other examples).

】

文档摘要：人工智能热潮中的重大增值

C# 开发人员经常需要跨企业系统构建大量文档的处理工作流程。当我们思考“文档处理”的含义时，我们可能会关注提取、验证、转换、存储等内容，但在许多现代系统中，能够快速解析文档所包含的内容通常与解析其中的数据一样重要。

无论我们是通过上传工作流程处理 PDF，还是利用电子邮件服务器来处理入站电子邮件附件，甚至是处理来自移动设备的手动扫描图像，生成简短、可读的文档摘要都可以极大地改善下游系统和用户与这些文档的交互方式。简洁的摘要段落可以帮助处理从文档批准和审查到搜索索引或分类（以及许多其他示例）的任何事情。

item.title: 【The AI4Agile Practitioners Report 2026】2026 年 AI4Agile 从业者报告
item.description:
【

TL;DR: The AI4Agile Practitioners Report 2026

83% of Agile practitioners use AI, but most spend 10% or less of their time with it because they do not know where it fits. Our survey of 289 Agile practitioners identifies the real adoption barriers and shows where AI creates value you can act on. 

We asked 289 Agile practitioners how they use AI. Most of them barely do.

】

TL;DR：2026 年 AI4Agile 从业者报告

83% 的敏捷从业者使用 AI，但大多数人只花 10% 或更少的时间在上面，因为他们不知道它适合什么地方。我们对 289 名敏捷从业者进行的调查确定了真正的采用障碍，并显示了人工智能在哪些方面创造了您可以采取行动的价值。 

我们询问了 289 名敏捷实践者如何使用人工智能。他们中的大多数人几乎不这样做。

item.title: 【Azure SLM Showdown: Evaluating Phi-3, Llama 3, and Snowflake Arctic for Production】Azure SLM 对决：评估 Phi-3、Llama 3 和 Snowflake Arctic 的生产情况
item.description:
【

In the rapidly evolving landscape of Generative AI, the industry is witnessing a significant shift. While the “bigger is better” mantra once dominated, the tide is turning. As organizations move from experimental pilots to production-grade applications, the focus has shifted toward small language models (SLMs). These models offer lower latency, reduced compute costs, and the ability to run on edge devices, while maintaining performance that rivals massive models like GPT-4 for specific tasks.

Microsoft Azure has positioned itself as a premier destination for these models, offering them through the Model-as-a-Service (MaaS) framework and the Azure AI Model Catalog. In this article, we provide a technical deep dive into three of the most prominent SLMs available on Azure: Microsoft’s Phi-3, Meta’s Llama 3 (8B), and Snowflake Arctic. We analyze their architectures, benchmark performance, deployment strategies, and cost efficiency to help you decide which model best fits your workload.

】

在快速发展的生成式 AI 领域，整个行业正在经历重大转变。虽然“越大越好”的口号曾经占据主导地位，但潮流正在发生转变。随着组织从实验性试点转向生产级应用程序，重点已转向小语言模型 (SLM)。这些模型提供更低的延迟、更低的计算成本以及在边缘设备上运行的能力，同时在特定任务方面保持与 GPT-4 等大规模模型相媲美的性能。

Microsoft Azure 将自己定位为这些模型的首要目的地，通过模型即服务 (MaaS) 框架和 Azure AI 模型目录提供这些模型。在本文中，我们从技术角度深入探讨了 Azure 上可用的三种最著名的 SLM：Microsoft 的 Phi-3、Meta 的 Llama 3 (8B) 和 Snowflake Arctic。我们分析他们的架构、基准性能、部署策略和成本效率，以帮助您决定哪种模型最适合您的工作负载。

item.title: 【Supply Chain Security for Tools and Prompts】工具和提示的供应链安全
item.description:
【

It's very easy to talk about secure GenAI. But did you ever think about whether your agents are running only the prompts, tool schemas, router rules, and semantic models you intended — especially after many weeks of rapid iteration? It is very hard to prove this. Most teams freeze application code and container images, but they leave one thing open: the fast-moving agent configuration supply chain.

Prompts and tool definitions change more often in production. Router rules are updated daily. Tool schemas get expanded just to add one field. A semantic model gets tweaked to fix a metric edge case. These types of edits can silently weaken policy controls, enable data-exfiltration paths, or simply reduce faithfulness until you are shipping wrong answers with confidence. For governed, trustworthy AI data systems, you must treat agent configuration like top-tier software artifacts, such as reviewing, signing, versioning, promoting, and auditing, and not like loose text files in a repository.

】

谈论安全 GenAI 非常容易。但是您是否想过您的代理是否仅运行您想要的提示、工具架构、路由器规则和语义模型，尤其是在经过数周的快速迭代之后？证明这一点非常困难。大多数团队都会冻结应用程序代码和容器映像，但他们会留下一件事：快速移动的代理配置供应链。

提示和工具定义在生产中更频繁地更改。路由器规则每天更新。工具模式的扩展只是为了添加一个字段。语义模型经过调整以修复度量边缘情况。这些类型的编辑可以默默地削弱策略控制，启用数据泄露路径，或者只是降低可信度，直到您放心地提供错误的答案。对于受治理、值得信赖的 AI 数据系统，您必须将代理配置视为顶级软件工件，例如审查、签名、版本控制、升级和审核，而不是像存储库中的松散文本文件一样。
